page: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, with a 40 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 56.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 459 GB of local storage and does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates that the GS4-4 exhibits modest processing capabilities. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score is average, uncached large-block memory write bandwidth is poor, falling into the bottom 10%, and read bandwidth is weak. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression, also register in the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds are low, with a medium 7B model achieving only 10.0 tokens per second for prompt processing.

The primary tradeoff of the GS4-4 is its massive memory capacity contrasted against its legacy CPU performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for workloads that require large memory footprints but have low computational demands. Appropriate workloads include in-memory caching, legacy database hosting, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require high-speed CPU throughput or modern vector instruction sets.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features an exceptionally high memory allocation of 224.0 GB (56.0 GB per core) alongside 459 GB of local storage. Benchmark results reveal a clear tradeoff: while memory capacity is substantial, processing power is limited, with stress-ng CPU performance and large-block memory write bandwidth falling into the bottom 10%. PassMark memory performance remains average, but database operations and web serving throughput are weak. Qualitatively, the GS4-4 provides high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as in-memory caching, legacy databases, and basic data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage, offering high memory density for workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 459 GB local storage, designed for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine pairing substantial RAM and local storage with modest compute capabilities for memory-intensive legacy workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Extremely high memory capacity of 224.0 GB (56.0 GB per core)
Includes 459 GB of local storage for data caching
Average memory performance contrasted by lower CPU benchmark scores
Well-suited for in-memory databases and memory-heavy legacy applications
